title: mertensia viridis
intro: Mertensia viridis, also known as bluebells or lungwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae) that typically grows in damp, shady areas. Traditionally, Mertensia viridis has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat respiratory issues, due to its high concentration of mucilages, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes. The health benefits of Mertensia viridis include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and alleviating symptoms of bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Mertensia viridis often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in ointments and salves.
